title: Register now: The energy imperative driving the push toward orbital data centers
summary: A virtual event on March 31, sponsored by Star Catcher and the Commercial Space Federation, will discuss the energy-driven push for orbital data centers. The event highlights the growing interest in space-based infrastructure to address energy challenges.

title: Kratos wins $446 million Space Force contract for missile-tracking ground systems
summary: Kratos secured a $446 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop ground management and integration systems for the Resilient Missile Warning and Tracking satellite program. This deal highlights the growing focus on missile defense and space-based tracking capabilities.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space plan debris removal service
summary: Portal Space Systems is partnering with Australian startup Paladin Space to develop a commercial orbital debris removal service using maneuverable spacecraft. This collaboration aims to address the growing issue of space debris in Earth's orbit.

title: Eileen Collins on what it takes to become Space Shuttle Commander
summary: Eileen Collins, the first woman to pilot the Space Shuttle, discusses her career and the requirements to become a Space Shuttle Commander in a Space Minds podcast interview. The episode is featured on SpaceNews.

title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in robotic lunar landing missions, which has sparked both excitement and confusion within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI. The deal marks a significant transaction in the satellite manufacturing and asset-financing sectors.

title: Apex sells satellite for Japanese technology demonstration mission
summary: Apex has secured a contract with a Japanese company to supply a satellite bus for a technology demonstration mission. This deal marks Apex's expansion into the Japanese space market.

title: Rocket Lab wins $190 million Pentagon deal for hypersonic test flights
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract from the Pentagon to conduct 20 hypersonic test flights over four years. This deal highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting U.S. defense and hypersonic technology development.

title: Space Command classified wargame to include 50 commercial players
summary: Space Command is conducting a classified wargame involving 50 commercial players to improve collaboration and understand commercial challenges. The exercise aims to enhance joint operations between Space Command and the commercial sector.

title: TransAstra aims to move 100-ton asteroid to stable orbit for processing
summary: TransAstra is studying the feasibility of moving a 100-ton asteroid to a stable near-Earth orbit for processing and research. The project aims to establish a robotic outpost for materials processing and manufacturing in space.

title: Earthshine will reveal the moon's hidden face this week — here's how to see it
summary: Earthshine will be visible this week, offering a glimpse of the moon's dark side. The phenomenon occurs when sunlight reflects off Earth to illuminate the moon.

title: Private South Korean rocket exploded last December due to hardware failure, investigation finds
summary: South Korean startup Innospace's first orbital launch attempt failed due to a hardware failure, resulting in the explosion of its private rocket last December. The launch, which carried five satellites, took place from Brazil on December 22.

title: Lego's first-ever Tintin set is coming next month and it's a space-themed Lego Tintin Moon rocket
summary: Lego is releasing its first-ever Tintin-themed set, a space-themed Lego Tintin Moon rocket, featuring six minifigures, available for purchase starting April 1.

title: I talked to Andy Weir about the astrobiology behind 'Project Hail Mary'
summary: The article discusses the astrobiology behind 'Project Hail Mary,' exploring the scientific plausibility of alien life as depicted in the new film. It features insights from author Andy Weir, known for 'The Martian.'

title: NASA peers inside an asteroid | Space photo of the day for March 19, 2026
summary: NASA has utilized advanced imaging to examine asteroid Bennu samples, revealing extensive networks of cracks within the rock particles. This discovery provides new insights into the asteroid's structure and composition.

title: Ohio fireball may have dropped meteorites between Akron and Canton — should you pick it up if you find one?
summary: A rare meteor exploded over Ohio with the force of 250 tons of TNT, potentially dropping meteorites between Akron and Canton. Experts discuss whether individuals should retrieve found meteorites.

title: An AI cyberattack could trigger a satellite apocalypse in the next 2 years. Are we prepared?
summary: AI-powered cyberattacks could potentially hijack satellites, leading to collisions and a cascade of debris that could make Earth's orbit unsafe within the next two years. Experts warn that current defenses may not be sufficient to prevent such a 'satellite apocalypse.'

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese 'Strix' satellite to orbit on March 20
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese radar satellite named 'Strix' into orbit on March 20. The launch can be watched live.

title: You can stream the two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes when travelling, save 73% and claim a free $50 voucher when you sign up for two years of NordVPN
summary: Two missing 'Doctor Who' episodes will be available on BBC iPlayer in the UK. NordVPN is offering a 73% discount and a $50 voucher for a two-year subscription.

title: Watch NASA roll out Artemis 2 moon rocket tonight ahead of April 1 launch
summary: NASA is set to roll out the Artemis 2 moon rocket to the launch pad on March 19 ahead of its planned April 1 launch. The event will be broadcast live for public viewing.

title: American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida documented a bald eagle flying away from its nest during annual nesting surveys. The surveys aim to track active and inactive bald eagle nests across Kennedy Space Center, Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge, and Canaveral National Seashore.

title: How NASA is Collecting Explosion Data for Next Generation Rockets
summary: NASA is gathering explosion data to enhance the safety of next-generation rockets, particularly those using liquid oxygen and methane propellants. Engineers with extensive cryogenic and test operations experience are leading this effort as propulsion technology scales up.

title: NASA Laser Reflecting Instrument Makes GPS Satellite More Accurate
summary: NASA's laser reflecting technology, launched aboard GPS III SV-09, is now operational and enhances GPS accuracy. The instrument, called a laser retroreflector array (LRA), was deployed on January 27 as part of the U.S. Space Force's Block III GPS satellite series.

title: NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking
summary: NASA will broadcast the launch and docking of Roscosmos' Progress 94 cargo spacecraft, which is delivering supplies to the International Space Station. The unpiloted spacecraft is set to launch on March 22.

title: Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class
summary: NASA's newest astronaut candidate class, selected in 2025, has been named 'the Platypi' and is training at Johnson Space Center for future missions to the ISS, Moon, and Mars.

title: NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has successfully completed final prelaunch tests, including extreme sound exposure, shaking, and electronic checks, confirming it can withstand launch conditions and function in space. The mission remains on track for an early launch.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Abundant rainfall in February and March 2026 turned the desert landscape of Central Australia green. This environmental change highlights the impact of weather patterns on arid regions.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4832–4837: Driving the (Contact) Line!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is in the final phase of its boxwork campaign, studying the contacts between the boxwork unit and the layered sulfate unit on Mars. The mission is led by the University of New Brunswick, Canada, with planning dates from March 13, 2026.

title: Lava Flows Down Mayon
summary: NASA's Landsat 8 captured a rare clear image of Mayon volcano in the Philippines on Feb. 26, 2026, highlighting lava flows using infrared observations. PHIVOLCS reported volcanic earthquakes, rockfalls, and ash clouds during the event.

title: From Service to Space Systems: A Pathways Journey to NASA
summary: Corey Elmore transitioned from military service to a NASA Pathways engineering internship at Kennedy Space Center, gaining hands-on experience in engineering environments and technical tools.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. The test brings the next Starship launch one step closer to reality.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine launches expected, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The mission concept highlights innovative approaches to planetary defense.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for lunar and Martian missions, highlighted by the transformation of its Starfactory facility to support the Starship program. The company is repurposing steel production infrastructure to advance its space exploration goals.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: The International Space Station (ISS) welcomed new crew members in February following the early departure of Crew-11 in mid-January due to a medical concern. The month was marked by new crew arrivals and highlighted scientific activities aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 expected to dominate the launch schedule. This highlights the rocket's frequent and pivotal role in current space missions.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a significant milestone since Flight 11 in October 2025. This development signals a new phase for the company's operations.

title: Firefly Alpha FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” launches successfully
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Alpha rocket (FLTA007 'Stairway to Seven') after a nearly 10-month pause, marking its return to flight operations.

title: Baikonur Launch Pad restored, clearing path for Progress MS-33 ISS Mission
summary: Baikonur Cosmodrome's Site 31/6 launch pad has been fully restored, enabling the upcoming Progress MS-33 mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This repair marks a critical step in maintaining Russia's ISS resupply operations.

title: NASA’s Artemis ‘Course Correction’ Boosts Moonward Momentum: Key Details Still Maturing
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced significant changes to the Artemis program to restore its momentum, though key details are still being finalized.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry-related topic. The specific details of the news were not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is now operational.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25 decisions with industry stakeholders. These events will outline planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in ESA's Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky and expensive solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun experiments using ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates microgravity conditions for research. This initiative supports educational and scientific exploration in space technology.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on a debris removal service to make it operational rather than experimental. The initiative aims to maintain critical satellite infrastructure supporting communications, navigation, weather forecasting, and national security.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its second suborbital flight of the SR75 rocket, scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first satellite bus contract in Japan with NEC Corporation. Under the agreement, NEC will purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission scheduled for next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket is set to launch this summer following modifications to improve nozzle and solid rocket booster margins. Changes were planned before an earlier anomaly and are now being accelerated.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to develop technology aimed at reducing reliance on satellite infrastructure.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics secured a national security deal with South Korea, marking another success in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the updated five-year-old regulations. No entities remain under the legacy licensing framework.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to meet rising demand, but policymakers can accelerate growth according to industry groups.

title: Nvidia Unveils New Hardware to Unlock Orbital Data Centers
summary: Nvidia has introduced new hardware designed to enable the development of orbital data centers, which could revolutionize space-based computing. This advancement marks a significant step toward making space-based data processing a reality.

title: Canadian Launchers and Spaceport Get Funding Boost
summary: Canada will invest in a spaceport and three young rocket systems to enhance its sovereign launch capabilities. This funding boost aims to strengthen the country's space industry.

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI is set to purchase two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by both companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. The company announced the delivery in a recent press release.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump's Cyber Strategy for America, outlining six policy pillars including securing critical infrastructure and advancing emerging technologies like crypto, AI, and quantum computing.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: A panel at Space-Comm in London discussed the security of UK space assets and strategic stability from government and industry perspectives.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract with the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. The contract focuses on enhancing security and reliability in UAS systems.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the UK discussed the future of warfare during an event at Chatham House, marking four years since Russia's invasion. Valerii Zaluzhnyi highlighted key aspects of modern conflict.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has assured the FCC that its SkyTerra Next proposal, involving an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, will not cause interference. The company filed a defense to the FCC, emphasizing compliance with existing L-band coordination.

title: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics to Build Solar Arrays for K-LEO
summary: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics will jointly build solar arrays for K-LEO, South Korea's national security LEO constellation. The collaboration was announced in a joint statement.

title: Connectivity Under Fire: The Critical Need for Satcom Terminal Survivability
summary: The article discusses the critical need for survivable satellite communication terminals in contested environments for U.S. military operations. It highlights the importance of beyond-line-of-sight connectivity for command, control, and safety-of-life communications.

title: Satellogic Plans Next-Gen Merlin EO Constellation
summary: Satellogic revealed plans for its next-generation 'Merlin' Earth observation satellites, offering one-meter resolution and daily mapping capabilities. The company aims to enhance its Earth observation constellation with this new technology.
